name: Sekoliko
description: Open source school management web application for school and university operations, including fee management, reporting, attendance, and administration workflows.
madagascar_relevance: The project README explicitly describes Sekoliko as a SaaS app for schools and universities in Madagascar, making it directly relevant to local education administration needs.
notes: The repository README and GitHub topics indicate a Symfony-based school management platform. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Madagascar Map
description: Open dataset repository listing Malagasy regions, districts, communes, towns, and fokontany, with JSON files intended for reuse in applications and APIs.
madagascar_relevance: The repository provides structured administrative and locality data for Madagascar, including regions, districts, communes, and fokontany, which is directly useful for local mapping, public-service, delivery, and civic applications.
notes: The repository provides a reusable territorial reference layer for Madagascar. A visible code push on 2024-01-08 supports a 'stable' catalogue status even without signs of heavy ongoing feature work.

name: PCG 2005
description: Structured data repository for the PCG 2005 chart of accounts used in Malagasy accounting.
madagascar_relevance: The repository contains structured data for the Madagascar PCG 2005 accounting framework, which is directly relevant for local accounting, bookkeeping, ERP, and financial software.
notes: The repository README describes the project as structured PCG 2005 data for Madagascar. License is marked 'unknown' because a reusable license could not be confidently verified from the repository page reviewed. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Gasy Corpus Crawler
description: Python crawler toolkit for scraping Malagasy-language websites and building text corpora for dataset creation.
madagascar_relevance: The repository is specifically designed to scrape Malagasy-language websites and build Malagasy text corpora, making it directly useful for local NLP, language modeling, and corpus creation work.
notes: The README presents the project as a set of scripts for corpus scraping with both BeautifulSoup and Selenium variants. The small commit history and script-oriented design suggest an experimental maturity level.

name: MMCLI
description: Command-line Malagasy encyclopedia and dictionary client that queries Malagasyword.org and returns searchable word data.
madagascar_relevance: The tool is built specifically around Malagasy dictionary and encyclopedia lookup, which makes it directly relevant to Malagasy language access and language-tooling workflows.
notes: The README states that the latest release is the only stable version and that the rest remains a work in progress with unresolved issues, so 'experimental' is the safest catalogue status.

name: Mada CLI
description: CLI and companion web tooling for searching Malagasy regions, districts, communes, and fokontany.
madagascar_relevance: The project is built around Madagascar's administrative and locality structure, including regions, districts, communes, and fokontany, which is directly useful for local search, address handling, and territorial data workflows.
notes: The repository shows visible maintenance through 2026 and combines reusable territorial lookup with a developer-friendly CLI, making it a strong early catalogue entry.

name: Rakibolana Malagasy
description: Dataset repository containing Malagasy words and their meanings in CSV, JSON, and SQLite formats.
madagascar_relevance: The repository contains Malagasy lexical data and definitions, which is directly useful for dictionary tooling, search, NLP resources, and broader Malagasy language preservation work.
notes: The repository README says the data is free to use and provides CSV, JSON, and SQLite exports, but a formal open source license could not be confidently verified from the repository page reviewed. Status is marked 'unknown' because current maintenance level was not confidently verified during manual review.

name: Pocketenv
description: Open sandbox runtime for agents and humans that provides portable development environments across multiple backend providers.
framework: Multi-backend sandbox CLI and SDK platform
madagascar_relevance: The repository was identified as being built by Malagasy developers, which makes it a relevant developer tool contribution from the local ecosystem and a reusable building block for Madagascar-based software teams.
notes: The README describes Pocketenv as a universal sandbox runtime for agents and humans and explicitly warns that it is still in early development, so 'experimental' is the safest catalogue status.

name: Awesome Madagascar
description: Curated Markdown list of projects, tools, websites, and resources related to Madagascar.
madagascar_relevance: The repository serves as a discovery layer for Madagascar-related projects and helps maintain ongoing ecosystem visibility across multiple technical domains.
notes: The repository is close to this catalogue's mission but complementary rather than duplicative, making it a strong source for watchlist-style curation.

name: Madagascar Map API
description: PHP API layer built on top of Madagascar Map data for querying Malagasy territorial information.
madagascar_relevance: The project exposes Madagascar administrative and locality data through an API layer, which is directly useful for civic, institutional, and logistics integrations.
notes: A visible release and repository activity around January 2024 support a 'stable' status. License is marked 'unknown' because a reusable open source license could not be confidently verified from the repository metadata reviewed.

name: Zip Code Madagascar
description: Small JSON dataset of Madagascar postal codes for validation and address-oriented forms.
madagascar_relevance: The dataset is directly useful for Madagascar-specific address validation, lookup, and form completion workflows.
notes: The repository is simple and immediately reusable. Its last visible code push was on 2022-05-24, so 'inactive' is safer than assuming active maintenance.

name: Botjagwar
description: Python automation scripts for Wiktionary workflows with direct relevance to Malagasy lexical infrastructure.
madagascar_relevance: The project helps automate dictionary-oriented workflows around Malagasy lexical resources, which is directly valuable for local language tooling and knowledge preservation.
notes: Visible repository activity continued into late 2025, making 'active' appropriate for the catalogue.

name: Malagasy TTS
description: Browser-based Malagasy text-to-speech demo built with Transformers.js.
madagascar_relevance: The project is a rare open experiment focused on Malagasy speech synthesis, which makes it strategically important for local language AI and accessibility work.
notes: The repository is promising but still small and prototype-like, so 'experimental' is the safest status despite recent visible activity.

name: Signaleo
description: Mobile application for sharing street, traffic, and carpooling information in Madagascar.
madagascar_relevance: The project is strongly localized around everyday mobility and congestion-sharing needs in Madagascar, which makes it a good fit for both mobility and civic-tech discovery.
notes: The repository remains valuable as a localized mobility reference, but its last visible code push was on 2023-11-12, so 'inactive' is the safest catalogue status.

name: Deforestation Maps Mada
description: Reproducible research repository documenting sixty years of deforestation and forest fragmentation in Madagascar.
madagascar_relevance: The repository is directly centered on Madagascar forest change and provides reusable environmental analysis assets with clear local scientific value.
notes: The work is highly valuable and reproducible, but the last visible code push was on 2020-11-25, so 'inactive' is the safest status.

name: CNTEMAD Repository
description: Student-driven repository for CNTEMAD learners to share course materials, examples, and peer support resources.
madagascar_relevance: The repository is tightly linked to a visible Malagasy student community and supports practical local knowledge-sharing in higher education.
notes: The project remains highly visible and useful, but it is best catalogued as 'inactive' because it is no longer actively maintained.

name: Imaginarium
description: AI-assisted platform for Malagasy cultural heritage education, centered on proverbs, oral tradition, and exploratory language experiences.
madagascar_relevance: The project is built specifically around Malagasy cultural content and language exploration, making it directly relevant to Malagasy language preservation, heritage education, and local AI-assisted discovery workflows.
notes: The public repository includes a clear MIT license and recent visible activity in May 2026. Its focus on ohabolana and other Malagasy oral-tradition content gives it clear secondary relevance to culture-history.

name: Fataplus Tsena ny Tantsaha
description: Flutter mobile application branded Fataplus-Tsena; the public repository context is sparse, but the project appears intended for Malagasy market and pricing workflows.
madagascar_relevance: The project name Tsena ny Tantsaha, app branding, and fata.plus backend references suggest a Madagascar-oriented local market application aimed at Malagasy users.
notes: The repository does not currently include a clear open source license file, and its public README remains a default Flutter starter README. The catalogue entry should be revisited if upstream documentation becomes more explicit.
